revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[ja:平野塚穴山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hirano Kofun cluster | native_name =平野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hirano Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.566944444444445|N|135.69472222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kashiba|qid=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Hirano Kofun Group|qid=Q72342556|1=Hirano Kofun Group}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|01|N|135|41|41|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Hirano Kofun cluster'''|平野古墳群|}} is a cluster of at least six [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Hirano neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kashiba, Nara|en|Kashiba, Nara|qid=Q696643|lt=Kashiba|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, [[Nara prefecture]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. One of these tumuli, the {{nihongo|'''Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun'''|平野塚穴山古墳|}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2022|title=平野塚穴山古墳 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hirano Kofun cluster is located on the south slope of a ridge that branches off to the east from the northeastern foot of Mount Futakami, which forms the border between {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} and Nara Prefecture. Six ''kofun'' have been confirmed so far; however, currently, only three (Kofun No. 1, 2, and 5) remain. On the northern slope of the same hill as the Hirano Kofun Group, Hirano Kilns 1–3, which were the first to fire {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery in the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, and Hirano Kilns 4–5, which fired Sue pottery and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s, have been identified. To the north of that are the {{ill|Ninji temple ruins|en|Ninji temple ruins|qid=Q105811232|lt=Ninji temple ruins|1=Ninji temple ruins}}, with a separate National Historic Site designation.
Currently, the Hirano Kofun cluster site has been developed into the Hirano Tsukaanayama Tomb Historic Site Park, and is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shizumi Station|en|Shizumi Station|qid=Q3655904|lt=Shizumi Station|1=Shizumi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|qid=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun=== Kofun No.5 in the Hirano Kofun cluster is named the "Tsukaanayama Kofun". It is a square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with a side length of about 21 meters and a height of about 4 meters. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is made from cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones, with an entrance to the south, and the interior is 1.75 meters high and is divided into an antechamber (3.05 meters long, 1.5 meters wide) and an entrance passage (0.8 meters long, 1.4 meters wide), with a gate (0.5 meters long, 1.2 meters wide) between them. The floor of the chamber is made up of 21 small cut stones, one for the back wall, two for each side wall, and two for the ceiling stone, and the entire inside of the chamber has been carefully polished. It has been open since ancient times, and appears in a {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} period map depicting it in this state, so the details of any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is unknown. This same map identifies the tumulus as the grave of {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzō|1=Emperor Kenzō}}, although this identification is not supported by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1972, during which time coffin fragments made of overlapping lacquered cloth were found. In addition, dd fragments of copper products, including one earring and one hollow ball, as well as fragments of what is believed to be a copper pot, were excavated, as well as a lacquered basket made of woven hemp and coated with lacquer, and a lacquer basket made of cloth and coated with lacquer. From the structure of the mound and these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed at the very end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Hirano Kofun No.2 and No.1=== Of the group of tombs, Hirano Kofun No. 2 has a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] measuring approximately 10.6 meters in length, making it one of the largest of the late Kofun tombs in the [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District|Nara|Kitakitsuragi]] region. This tomb is unique in that part of the burial chamber is made of tuff (mostly {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} from {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|qid=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}}, whereas the coffin base is made of earthen bricks and a support stand. Hirano Kofun No. 1 also has a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber made entirely of Mount Nijō tuff, including the Sarcophagus, which is made out of a hollowed-out block of granite. These tumuli are both {{nihongo|''enfun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameters of 26 meters, and both are also estimated to have been built in the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. No previous construction of tombs is known in this area, and the group of tombs suddenly began to be built in the 7th century. It is believed that these tumuli are the tombs of the Kayano clan, descendants of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], who ruled the region during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Hirano Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Hirano Kurumazuka Kofun（Kofun No.1） File:Hirano Kurumazuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Hirano Kurumazuka Kofun Burial Chamber（Kofun No.1） File:Hirano Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|Hirano Kofun No.2 File:Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun.jpg|Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun File:Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekkaku-2.jpg|Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun Burial Chamber </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}

==External links== {{Commons Category:dd]]{{template-inline|Hirano Kofun Group}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main01001.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/soshiki/15/5975.html Kashiba city home page]{{in lang|ja}}
